uses: Spirit runeword shield (Tal + Thul + Ort + Amn)
Splendor, Sanctuary, Exile (ethereal only for Exile)
demand_drivers: Spirit Monarch is the default endgame shield for caster builds
low strength requirement (156) for a 4-socket shield
supply_notes: dropped by many monsters in Hell difficulty
Monarch with 4 sockets can be Larzuk quest reward or cube recipe
white Monarch is common; 4os is common from cube or Larzuk

important_rolls_or_variants: superior vs non-superior — superior may have enhanced defense or durability
ethereal 4os Monarch is only useful for Exile runeword (low demand)
segment_sensitivity: ladder vs non-ladder: moderate price difference in early ladder; minimal later
early ladder: 4os Monarch is significantly more expensive
low-value item — pricing noise may dominate
known_model_risks: low absolute price means small Ist fractions — may need FG or PGem unit
superior vs non-superior may have small price difference
white (0os) Monarch trades differently than 4os Monarch — must distinguish

traderie.notes: High volume for a base item. Need to verify listings distinguish 0os vs 4os reliably.

reddit.notes: Use for demand, sentiment, confusion points, source discovery, and item-profile updates.

rmt_sites.notes: External cash-market signal only. Do not present as a recommendation to buy or sell.

research_notes.note: Initial draft profile. 4os Monarch is the most commonly traded runeword base. Good candidate for base-item pricing.

open_questions: Does Traderie reliably distinguish 0-socket Monarch from 4-socket Monarch?
Is 4os Monarch volume high enough for VWAP in all segments?
Should ethereal 4os Monarch be priced separately?
